rationale: The cited article is a broad tutorial-style review of behavioral economics models, while the citing paper is a bid-level empirical reverse-auction study using reduced-form and Bayesian adaptive-belief estimators; the notes do not explicitly indicate that the six reviewed models are central to the citing paper’s identification or estimation. With only a single DOI match and no in-text citation context, the citation could be background framing or a substantive theory source, so it cannot be safely dropped.
